content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012
July 23, 1986

Mr. Paul Sarvis, A3-185/14-2
McDonnell Douglas Astronautics
5301 Bolsa Avenue
Huntington Beach, California 92647

Re: Adv. File A86-230

Dear Mr. Sarvis:

Pursuant to your request, this letter is to confirm our
telephone conversation of Tuesday, July 15, 1986 concerning
semi-annual statements for a recipient committee in 1985 and
in 1986.

I stated that in 1985, if a recipient committee did not
receive contributions, nor made any expenditures during the
six month period covered by the semi-annual statement, the
recipient committee did not have to file a semi-annual
campaign statement.

The second semi-annual statement for 1985 covered the
period of July 1 through December 31, 1985 and was due no
later than January 31, 1986 and was subject to the campaign
provisions as of January 1, 1985.

In 1986, because of legislative changes effective
January 1, 1986, I stated that all recipient committees shall
file semi-annual statements each year no later than July 31
the period ending June 30, and no later than January 31 for
the period ending December 31 (Government Code Sections 84200
(a)).

I have enclosed a copy of the Political Reform of 1974
as Amended to January 1, 1986 for your convenience.

If I can be of any further assistance, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

HELEN P. ARRIOLA
Political Reform Consultant

HPA:cv
Enclosure

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a recipient committee must file semi-annual statements if it receives no contributions or makes no expenditures during the reporting period, and what filing deadlines apply in 1985 versus 1986 under new legislative changes.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: In 1985, recipient committees with no contributions or expenditures during a semi-annual period were not required to file; however, starting January 1, 1986, all recipient committees must file semi-annual statements by July 31 (for period ending June 30) and January 31 (for period ending December 31) regardless of activity level.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; advice provided through body paragraphs of letter. Document appears to be a confirmation letter following a telephone conversation rather than a response to a written request. Some OCR issues with dates and formatting noted.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a recipient committee must file semi-annual statements if it receives no contributions or makes no expenditures during the reporting period, and what filing deadlines apply in 1985 versus 1986 under new legislative changes.

CONCLUSION: In 1985, recipient committees with no contributions or expenditures during a semi-annual period were not required to file; however, starting January 1, 1986, all recipient committees must file semi-annual statements by July 31 (for period ending June 30) and January 31 (for period ending December 31) regardless of activity level.

File A86-230 Dear Mr. Sarvis: Pursuant to your request, this letter is to confirm our telephone conversation of Tuesday, July 15, 1986 concerning semi-annual statements for a recipient committee in 1985 and in 1986. I stated that in 1985, if a recipient committee did not receive contributions, nor made any expenditures during the six month period covered by the semi-annual statement, the recipient committee did not have to file a semi-annual campaign statement. The second semi-annual statement for 1985 covered the period of July 1 through December 31, 1985 and was due no later than January 31, 1986 and was subject to the campaign provisions as of January 1, 1985. In 1986, because of legislative changes effective January 1, 1986, I stated that all recipient committees shall file semi-annual statements each year no later than July 31 the period ending June 30, and no later than January 31 for the period ending December 31 (Government Code Sections 84200 (a)). I have enclosed a copy of the Political Reform of 1974 as Amended to January 1, 1986 for your convenience. If I can be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, HELEN P. ARRIOLA Political Reform Consultant HPA:cv Enclosure SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming filing requirements for recipient committee semi-annual statements, noting a 1986 legislative change requiring all committees to file regardless of whether they received contributions or made expenditures.
